title: convolvulus lanuginosus var. sericeus
intro: Convolvulus lanuginosus var. sericeus, also known as the silky bindweed or silky convolvulus, is a plant species in the morning glory family, characterized by its silky hairs and small, white flowers. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Convolvulus lanuginosus var. sericeus are attributed to its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate skin irritations. Convolvulus lanuginosus var. sericeus is commonly prepared as a tea or infusion, and can also be used in salves and ointments for topical application.
